clusters.title: Trump disputes Iran’s rejection of nuclear inspectors
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump disputes Iran’s rejection of nuclear inspectors
bullet_summary: nuclear inspectors into the country, the vice president said after a marathon round of negotiations in Switzerland
Reported by Al Jazeera, CBS News, BBC World and others

items.title: Trump disputes Iran’s rejection of nuclear inspectors
items.standfirst: President Trump pushed back on Iran’s foreign ministry claim that there are no plans to allow IAEA nuclear inspectors.
items.content: President Trump pushed back on Iran’s foreign ministry claim that there are no plans to allow IAEA nuclear inspectors.

items.title: Trump says Iran agreed to nuclear inspections, but Tehran denies claim
items.standfirst: Iran insists there are no plans for inspections of its bombed nuclear sites, but Trump says Tehran "fully and completely" agreed to let inspectors return.
items.content: Iran insists there are no plans for inspections of its bombed nuclear sites, but Trump says Tehran "fully and completely" agreed to let inspectors return.

items.title: Future of Iran's nuclear program uncertain amid peace talks
items.standfirst: The future of Iran's nuclear program remains unclear amid recent peace talks, despite President Trump's claims that the country "completely agreed" to nuclear inspections. CBS News' Courtney Kealy has more.
items.content: The future of Iran's nuclear program remains unclear amid recent peace talks, despite President Trump's claims that the country "completely agreed" to nuclear inspections. CBS News' Courtney Kealy has more.

items.title: Iran says no new commitments on nuclear sites after Vance says inspectors to be invited back
items.standfirst: Iran's foreign ministry says it made "no new commitments" on nuclear inspections after talks in Switzerland.
items.content: Iran's foreign ministry says it made "no new commitments" on nuclear inspections after talks in Switzerland.

items.title: U.S.-Iran talks set ‘good foundation’ for deal to end war, Vance says
items.standfirst: Tehran agreed to allow U.N. nuclear inspectors into the country, the vice president said after a marathon round of negotiations in Switzerland.
items.content: Tehran agreed to allow U.N. nuclear inspectors into the country, the vice president said after a marathon round of negotiations in Switzerland.

clusters.title: Portugal thrash Uzbekistan as Ronaldo scores twice to make history
clusters.neutral_headline: Ronaldo scores twice as Portugal thrash Uzbekistan 5-0 at World Cup 2026
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC Top Stories, Al Jazeera, France24 and others

items.title: Portugal thrash Uzbekistan as Ronaldo scores twice to make history
items.standfirst: Cristiano Ronaldo becomes the first player to score in six World Cups as his double helps Portugal thrash Uzbekistan.
items.content: Cristiano Ronaldo becomes the first player to score in six World Cups as his double helps Portugal thrash Uzbekistan.

items.title: Ronaldo scores twice as Portugal thrash Uzbekistan 5-0 at World Cup 2026
items.standfirst: Cristiano Ronaldo becomes first player to score at six World Cup tournaments as Portugal thrash debutants Uzbekistan.
items.content: Cristiano Ronaldo becomes first player to score at six World Cup tournaments as Portugal thrash debutants Uzbekistan.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Portugal and record-breaking Ronaldo outshine Uzbekistan
items.standfirst: Record-breaker Cristiano Ronaldo guided Portugal to a 5-0 win over Uzbekistan in World Cup Group K on Tuesday. Ronaldo became ​the first player to score in six ​World Cups and Portugal’s all-time leading marksman at the global finals with two goals in Houston.
items.content: Record-breaker Cristiano Ronaldo guided Portugal to a 5-0 win over Uzbekistan in World Cup Group K on Tuesday. Ronaldo became ​the first player to score in six ​World Cups and Portugal’s all-time leading marksman at the global finals with two goals in Houston.

items.title: Portugal's Ronaldo shakes off World Cup doubters, scores 2 after sluggish start
items.standfirst: At 41, Cristiano Ronaldo is one of the oldest players in the World Cup. Against the Democratic Republic of Congo he was mostly invisible and questions mounted. Against Uzbekistan, he sparkled.
items.content: At 41, Cristiano Ronaldo is one of the oldest players in the World Cup. Against the Democratic Republic of Congo he was mostly invisible and questions mounted. Against Uzbekistan, he sparkled.(Image credit: Michael Steele)

clusters.title: Senate adopts House-passed Iran resolution in symbolic rebuke of Trump
clusters.neutral_headline: US Senate votes to pass Iran war powers resolution, in blow to Trump
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, NBC World, Al Jazeera

items.title: Senate adopts House-passed Iran resolution in symbolic rebuke of Trump
items.standfirst: The Senate approved a House-passed resolution aimed at reining in President Trump on Iran, marking the first time such a measure has made it through both chambers.
items.content: The Senate approved a House-passed resolution aimed at reining in President Trump on Iran, marking the first time such a measure has made it through both chambers.

items.title: Trump touts Iran deal in Pennsylvania as Senate adopts war powers resolution in symbolic rebuke
items.standfirst: President Trump traveled to a Mack Trucks facility in Pennsylvania on Tuesday, touting his economic agenda and the Iran agreement. The event came as the Senate adopted the House-passed Iran war powers resolution. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has the latest.
items.content: President Trump traveled to a Mack Trucks facility in Pennsylvania on Tuesday, touting his economic agenda and the Iran agreement. The event came as the Senate adopted the House-passed Iran war powers resolution. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has the latest.

items.title: Senate rebukes Trump by approving House-passed measure calling for end to Iran war
items.standfirst: Passage of the largely symbolic measure comes as the U.S. and Iran are in the early stages of talks aimed at ending the war.
items.content: Passage of the largely symbolic measure comes as the U.S. and Iran are in the early stages of talks aimed at ending the war.

items.title: US Senate votes to pass Iran war powers resolution, in blow to Trump
items.standfirst: The resolution is the first to pass both the Senate and the House, though it is likely to face a presidential veto.
items.content: The resolution is the first to pass both the Senate and the House, though it is likely to face a presidential veto.

items.title: Watch: Trump takes questions on Iran during Pennsylvania visit
items.standfirst: President Trump spoke to reporters Tuesday before visiting a Mack Trucks facility in Pennsylvania.
items.content: President Trump spoke to reporters Tuesday before visiting a Mack Trucks facility in Pennsylvania.

clusters.title: Alibaba sues US military over labelling it a ‘Chinese military company’

bullet_summary: Reported by Al Jazeera, Financial Times, Bloomberg Technology

items.title: Alibaba sues US military over labelling it a ‘Chinese military company’
items.standfirst: E-commerce giant says that claims of affiliation with Chinese military have 'no basis in fact or law'.
items.content: E-commerce giant says that claims of affiliation with Chinese military have 'no basis in fact or law'.

items.title: Alibaba sues Pentagon over inclusion on Chinese military blacklist
items.standfirst: Ecommerce giant claims US defence department’s ‘arbitrary and capricious’ decision lacked evidence
items.content: Ecommerce giant claims US defence department’s ‘arbitrary and capricious’ decision lacked evidence

items.title: Alibaba Sues the US, Seeking Removal From Pentagon’s Blacklist
items.standfirst: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. sued the Department of Defense to be removed from a blacklist that identifies the e-commerce leader as supporter of the Chinese military, appealing to the US justice system to avoid a designation the company says is arbitrary and unjustified.
items.content: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. sued the Department of Defense to be removed from a blacklist that identifies the e-commerce leader as supporter of the Chinese military, appealing to the US justice system to avoid a designation the company says is arbitrary and unjustified.

items.content: President Trump says the pool is probably going to be drained again, two weeks after a multi-million dollar renovation was completed.

items.title: Trump says several people arrested for alleged vandalism of Reflecting Pool
items.standfirst: President Trump claims the problems with the Reflecting Pool in Washington are due to vandalism.
items.content: President Trump claims the problems with the Reflecting Pool in Washington are due to vandalism.

items.title: Trump says repairs to algae-plagued Reflecting Pool will begin immediately

items.content: Despite a multi-million dollar renovation, the Washington DC landmark has peeling paint and algae. Trump claims vandals are partly to blame.

clusters.title: What has Britain's likely next prime minister said about Trump?
clusters.neutral_headline: Why do the UK’s prime ministers keep resigning
bullet_summary: Andy Burnham, Britain's likely next leader, hasn't said a lot about President Trump, but his few statements have been critical
10 years to the day after the Brexit vote, Britain was looking to Andy Burnham as its likely next leader after Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation
British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced that he will resign, paving the way for the country’s 7th leader in a decade
British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced he would resign from office after facing a rebellion from members of his own party

items.title: What has Britain's likely next prime minister said about Trump?
items.standfirst: Andy Burnham, Britain's likely next leader, hasn't said a lot about President Trump, but his few statements have been critical.
items.content: Andy Burnham, Britain's likely next leader, hasn't said a lot about President Trump, but his few statements have been critical.

items.title: Why do the UK’s prime ministers keep resigning?
items.standfirst: Keir Starmer is the latest to step down, after less than two years in office.
items.content: Keir Starmer is the latest to step down, after less than two years in office.

items.title: 10 years after Brexit, Keir Starmer’s resignation highlights Britain’s deeper issues
items.standfirst: 10 years to the day after the Brexit vote, Britain was looking to Andy Burnham as its likely next leader after Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation.
items.content: 10 years to the day after the Brexit vote, Britain was looking to Andy Burnham as its likely next leader after Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation.

items.title: Andy Burnham could succeed Keir Starmer as next prime minister
items.standfirst: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced that he will resign, paving the way for the country’s 7th leader in a decade. NBC News' Molly Hunter reports on Starmer’s likely successor, Andy Burnham, the popular former mayor of Greater Manchester who secured a return to Parliament.
items.content: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced that he will resign, paving the way for the country’s 7th leader in a decade. NBC News' Molly Hunter reports on Starmer’s likely successor, Andy Burnham, the popular former mayor of Greater Manchester who secured a return to Parliament.

items.title: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announces resignation
items.standfirst: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced he would resign from office after facing a rebellion from members of his own party. Starmer became prime minister in 2024 after winning a landslide election.
items.content: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced he would resign from office after facing a rebellion from members of his own party. Starmer became prime minister in 2024 after winning a landslide election.

items.title: Keir Starmer resigns as British prime minister, clearing the path for the country’s 7th leader in a decade
items.standfirst: The announcement clears the path to power for Burnham, the popular Greater Manchester mayor.
items.content: The announcement clears the path to power for Burnham, the popular Greater Manchester mayor.

clusters.title: Top auto regulator investigates deadly Tesla crash into Texas home
clusters.neutral_headline: Top auto regulator investigates deadly Tesla crash into Texas home
bullet_summary: A Tesla driver on Friday said he was using ‘self-driving’ technology after his vehicle sped into a Texas home
Reported by Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: Top auto regulator investigates deadly Tesla crash into Texas home
items.standfirst: The vehicle was using an automated driving feature at high speed and killed a 76-year-old woman standing in the homeSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailThe top US auto regulator opened an investigation on Monday after a Tesla using an automated driving feature slammed into a Texas home at high speed and killed a 76-year-old woman standing inside.The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) said it’s opening a special investigation into the Tesla Model 3 crash on Friday near Houston, a significant investigation because the car was using technology that Elon Musk considers key to his company’s future. Continue reading...
items.content: The vehicle was using an automated driving feature at high speed and killed a 76-year-old woman standing in the homeSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailThe top US auto regulator opened an investigation on Monday after a Tesla using an automated driving feature slammed into a Texas home at high speed and killed a 76-year-old woman standing inside.The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) said it’s opening a special investigation into the Tesla Model 3 crash on Friday near Houston, a significant investigation because the car was using technology that Elon Musk considers key to his company’s future. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Israel continues to commit genocide by targeting children in Gaza, UN inquiry finds
clusters.neutral_headline: Israel continues to commit genocide by targeting children in Gaza, UN inquiry finds
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: Israel continues to commit genocide by targeting children in Gaza, UN inquiry finds
items.standfirst: Independent report says by aiming at children Israel is undermining capacity of Palestinian people to existIsrael continues to commit genocide by deliberately targeting Palestinian children in Gaza, an ⁠independent UN inquiry has found.The report by the UN independent international commission of inquiry examined violations against Palestinian children since the start of the war in Gaza, and said about 30% of the people killed by Israeli forces have been children. Continue reading...
items.content: Independent report says by aiming at children Israel is undermining capacity of Palestinian people to existIsrael continues to commit genocide by deliberately targeting Palestinian children in Gaza, an ⁠independent UN inquiry has found.The report by the UN independent international commission of inquiry examined violations against Palestinian children since the start of the war in Gaza, and said about 30% of the people killed by Israeli forces have been children. Continue reading...

items.title: UN commission of inquiry says Israel committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children
items.standfirst: Israel rejects the new report by the three-member expert panel, calling it a "libellous sham".
items.content: Israel rejects the new report by the three-member expert panel, calling it a "libellous sham".

clusters.title: Trump to attend World Cup final and present trophy

bullet_summary: Reported by BBC Top Stories, Guardian World

items.title: Trump to attend World Cup final and present trophy
items.standfirst: United States president Donald Trump will attend the World Cup final and help present the trophy, says Fifa president Gianni Infantino.
items.content: United States president Donald Trump will attend the World Cup final and help present the trophy, says Fifa president Gianni Infantino.

items.title: Donald Trump to present World Cup trophy to winners, says Gianni Infantino
items.standfirst: Two men due to share trophy-presenting on 19 July‘We are together all the time’, says Fifa president Donald Trump will hand over the World Cup trophy to the winners at the final on 19 July, Gianni Infantino has said.Infantino and Trump have forged a close relationship in the buildup to these finals, but the US president has made very few public pronouncements concerning the tournament since it began on 11 June. Continue reading...
items.content: Two men due to share trophy-presenting on 19 July‘We are together all the time’, says Fifa president Donald Trump will hand over the World Cup trophy to the winners at the final on 19 July, Gianni Infantino has said.Infantino and Trump have forged a close relationship in the buildup to these finals, but the US president has made very few public pronouncements concerning the tournament since it began on 11 June. bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, BBC World

items.title: Supreme Court rules ex-inmate can't sue prison officials for shaving dreadlocks
items.standfirst: The Supreme Court rejected a former Louisiana inmate's effort to sue state prison officials after they shaved his dreadlocks in violation of his religious beliefs.
items.content: The Supreme Court rejected a former Louisiana inmate's effort to sue state prison officials after they shaved his dreadlocks in violation of his religious beliefs.

items.title: Supreme Court bars Rastafarian man from suing prison officials who shaved dreadlocks
items.standfirst: In a 6-3 ruling, the Supreme Court ruled that a devout Rastafari man in Louisiana can't sue the state prison officials who forcibly shaved his dreadlocks. CBS News' chief legal correspondent Jan Crawford reports on the court's most recent and upcoming cases.
items.content: In a 6-3 ruling, the Supreme Court ruled that a devout Rastafari man in Louisiana can't sue the state prison officials who forcibly shaved his dreadlocks. CBS News' chief legal correspondent Jan Crawford reports on the court's most recent and upcoming cases.

items.title: US top court says Rastafarian man cannot sue prison guards who cut his dreadlocks
items.standfirst: The former Louisiana inmate argued his Rastafarian faith was violated after prison officials focibly shaved off his hair.
items.content: The former Louisiana inmate argued his Rastafarian faith was violated after prison officials focibly shaved off his hair.

clusters.title: France records hottest day ever as 40 people drown across country
clusters.neutral_headline: France records hottest day ever as 40 people drown across country
bullet_summary: “There is a tragic scourge of drownings,” prime minister Sébastien Lecornu said on Tuesday
Reported by Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: France records hottest day ever as 40 people drown across country
items.standfirst: National weather service, Météo-France, says Tuesday was the hottest day since measurements began in 1947Europe heatwave live – latest updatesFrance has registered its hottest day on record as 40 people across the country were confirmed to have drowned while swimming in unsupervised areas over the last few days.“There is a tragic scourge of drownings,” prime minister Sébastien Lecornu said on Tuesday. “The latest figures we’ve received are 40 deaths since 18 June. Most of the victims are young people.” Continue reading...
items.content: National weather service, Météo-France, says Tuesday was the hottest day since measurements began in 1947Europe heatwave live – latest updatesFrance has registered its hottest day on record as 40 people across the country were confirmed to have drowned while swimming in unsupervised areas over the last few days.“There is a tragic scourge of drownings,” prime minister Sébastien Lecornu said on Tuesday. “The latest figures we’ve received are 40 deaths since 18 June. Most of the victims are young people.” Continue reading...

items.title: Drowning deaths soar in France as Europe buckles in peak of heatwave
items.standfirst: Forty people have drowned in heatwave-related deaths in France since last Thursday, Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu says.
items.content: Forty people have drowned in heatwave-related deaths in France since last Thursday, Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu says.

clusters.title: Kenyan minister orders halt to construction of US Ebola facility
clusters.neutral_headline: Kenyan minister orders halt to construction of US Ebola facility
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: Kenyan minister orders halt to construction of US Ebola facility
items.standfirst: Decision comes after Aden Duale was held in contempt for ignoring previous high court ruling to stop work Kenya’s health minister told a court he had ordered preparations for a US-run Ebola quarantine facility to stop, after being held in contempt for ignoring a previous order to end work.Many Kenyans strongly oppose the facility, with deadly protests erupting since the complex was announced in May for US citizens evacuated from the Democratic Republic of Congo, which is grappling with a widespread Ebola outbreak. Continue reading...
items.content: Decision comes after Aden Duale was held in contempt for ignoring previous high court ruling to stop work Kenya’s health minister told a court he had ordered preparations for a US-run Ebola quarantine facility to stop, after being held in contempt for ignoring a previous order to end work.Many Kenyans strongly oppose the facility, with deadly protests erupting since the complex was announced in May for US citizens evacuated from the Democratic Republic of Congo, which is grappling with a widespread Ebola outbreak. Continue reading...
